hero.badge: Saigon, Cu Chi & Mekong Delta — 4 Days
hero.title: 4 Days in South Vietnam.
hero.titleAccent: City Buzz, Underground War History & Delta Life on the River.
hero.intro: Three completely different South Vietnams in four days. Saigon's kinetic streets and history-heavy museums. The Cu Chi Tunnels — 250 kilometres of hand-dug passages that sheltered an army underground during the American War. And the Mekong Delta's river life: sampan through coconut palm canals, tropical fruit orchards, folk music, and the coconut candy factories of Ben Tre.
hero.heroImageAlt: Mekong Delta sampan boat coconut palms Ben Tre Vietnam

includes: Hotel accommodation (3 nights Ho Chi Minh City)
Mekong Delta boat tour (My Tho and Ben Tre)
All sightseeing and entrance fees per itinerary

overview.heading: 4 Days South Vietnam: Saigon, Cu Chi & Mekong Delta
body: This four-day package covers the three South Vietnam experiences that define the region for most visitors: Ho Chi Minh City's urban energy and war history museums, the Cu Chi Tunnels' underground testament to the Vietnam War, and the Mekong Delta's river culture — a world of boat life, coconut palm canals, tropical fruit orchards, and village cottage industries.

Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon) is the largest and most energetic city in Vietnam. The War Remnants Museum presents the American War with unflinching directness and is one of the most visited museums in Southeast Asia. The Independence Palace — where General Duong Van Minh surrendered on 30 April 1975, ending the war — is still furnished as it was that day, including the war room, the underground bunkers, and the rooftop helipad. Notre Dame Cathedral and the Central Post Office are colonial-era landmarks within walking distance.

My Tho in the Mekong Delta, 70 kilometres from Ho Chi Minh City, is the gateway to the delta's river world. The day trip includes a boat ride to Thoi Son Island through coconut palm-lined waterways, tasting of tropical fruits and honey tea, traditional folk music, a sampan paddle under the palms, and a visit to Ben Tre village to see coconut candy production and rice paper manufacturing. It is a complete picture of the Mekong Delta in a single day.

highlights.heading: Why This 4-Day South Vietnam Package Stands Apart

items.title: Cu Chi Tunnels — Vietnam's Underground City
items.body: The Cu Chi Tunnels were hand-dug by Vietnamese resistance fighters beginning in the 1940s during the French war, and massively expanded by the Viet Cong during the American War. At their peak in the 1960s, the tunnel network extended 250 kilometres at three underground levels, containing kitchens, field hospitals, ammunition stores, and command centres. Up to 16,000 people lived underground in Ben Duoc and Ben Dinh for years at a time. Crawling through a widened tourist section of the tunnel — crouching through near-darkness — makes the history immediately visceral in a way no museum exhibit can replicate.
items.imageAlt: Cu Chi Tunnels Vietnam underground network American War history

items.title: Mekong Delta — River Life & Sampan Paddle
items.body: The Mekong Delta is the network of waterways, rice paddies, and islands formed by the Mekong River as it spreads into nine distributaries before reaching the South China Sea. My Tho and Ben Tre, the most accessible delta towns from Saigon, give a clear view of the river culture that has sustained the region for centuries: wooden sampan boats threading through coconut palm canals, floating markets on the water, cottage industries producing coconut candy and rice paper on wooden home presses, and village musicians playing Southern Vietnamese folk songs on traditional instruments.
items.imageAlt: Mekong Delta sampan boat coconut palm waterway My Tho Vietnam

items.title: War Remnants Museum — History Unflinchingly Told
items.body: The War Remnants Museum in District 3 is the most visited museum in Vietnam and one of the most powerful war museums in Asia. Its collection documents the American War through photographs, weaponry, military equipment, and accounts of civilian suffering that leave most visitors shaken. The outdoor exhibition of captured American military hardware — helicopters, tanks, artillery, and the infamous defoliation aircraft — fills the courtyard. The photograph galleries on the upper floors, including works by photographers who died covering the war, are among the most significant documentary collections in the world.
items.imageAlt: War Remnants Museum Ho Chi Minh City Saigon Vietnam history

items.title: Nguyen Hue Street & Café Apartment Block
items.body: The pedestrianised Nguyen Hue boulevard is modern Saigon's social centre — a wide walking street lined with the city's best coffee shops, restaurants, and the Ho Chi Minh City People's Committee Hall (the colonial-era Hôtel de Ville). The Café Apartment Block at 42 Nguyen Hue is a nine-storey residential building from the 1960s converted entirely into independent cafés, boutique shops, and creative studios, each occupying a single apartment and accessible by tiny vintage lifts. The building's external balconies, draped in plants and fairy lights, have become one of Saigon's most photographed streetscapes.
items.imageAlt: Nguyen Hue Street Café Apartment Saigon Ho Chi Minh City Vietnam

days.title: Arrive Ho Chi Minh City — City Tour
days.description: Arrive at Tan Son Nhat International Airport; met by your guide and transferred to your hotel. Lunch at a local restaurant. Saigon city tour: War Remnants Museum, Independence Palace (the building where the Vietnam War ended on 30 April 1975), Notre Dame Cathedral (with its famous red-brick façade sourced from Marseille), and Saigon Central Post Office (designed by Alfred Foulhoux in 1886 in Gothic-Renaissance style). Shopping at Saigon Square Market and Ben Thanh Market. Dinner at a local Vietnamese restaurant.

days.title: Cu Chi Tunnels & Nguyen Hue Street Walking Tour
days.description: After breakfast, depart for Cu Chi (approximately 40 km northwest, 90-minute drive). Visit the Ben Dinh or Ben Duoc Cu Chi Tunnel complex — your local guide explains the construction methods, the wartime daily life underground, and the military significance of the network. You can crawl through a widened section of the tunnel, inspect the trapdoor entrances, the ventilation systems, and the booby traps used to protect the perimeter. The outdoor exhibition area displays captured American military vehicles and weaponry. Lunch at a local restaurant. Return to Saigon for an afternoon guided walking tour: Nguyen Hue Street and the Café Apartment Block at 42 Nguyen Hue.
suggestedActivities: Cu Chi Tunnels (1.5 hour drive from HCMC)
Tunnel complex tour with local guide
Trap door and ventilation system exhibits
Café Apartment Block at 42 Nguyen Hue

days.title: Mekong Delta Day Trip — My Tho & Ben Tre
days.description: Early morning departure for My Tho, approximately 70 km from Ho Chi Minh City (about 1.5 hours through rice field landscapes). Boat ride from My Tho pier to Thoi Son Island through coconut palm-lined waterways. On the island: sample seasonal tropical fruits from local orchards, taste Vietnamese tea mixed with honey, and enjoy traditional Southern Vietnamese folk songs performed by local villagers. Sampan paddle under the coconut palms. Lunch at a local restaurant with authentic Mekong Delta cuisine. Continue to Ben Tre village for visits to local cottage industries: coconut candy production (watch the process from raw coconut to wrapped candy) and rice paper production for Vietnamese fresh rolls. Return to Ho Chi Minh City; drop-off at Takashimaya Shopping Centre for free shopping time. Transfer back to hotel.

days.title: Ho Chi Minh City Free Morning — Depart
days.description: Free morning in Ho Chi Minh City for self-arranged activities. Options include: Bui Vien Walking Street (Saigon's backpacker street, quieter in the morning), the Jade Emperor Pagoda (Phuoc Hai Tu — an extraordinary Taoist temple in District 3), Cholon (Saigon's Chinatown and the Thien Hau Pagoda), or final shopping at Ben Thanh Market. Transfer to Tan Son Nhat International Airport per your agreed schedule.
suggestedActivities: Free morning options: Jade Emperor Pagoda, Cholon, Ben Thanh

whyBook.heading: South Vietnam — Arranged by Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: Dhesu has been arranging South Vietnam journeys for Malaysian travellers since 1988. Our Ho Chi Minh City guides know how to navigate the city's history honestly and thoroughly — the Cu Chi Tunnels need a guide who can explain the context, not just point at the exhibits. Our Mekong Delta operators know which villages give the most authentic experience of delta life. Every day is arranged for genuine encounter, not tourist theatre.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. South Vietnam is one of our most booked destinations — we understand every aspect of the city-to-delta logistics.

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been sending Malaysian travellers to South Vietnam. Our HCMC guides and Mekong Delta operators are trusted long-term relationships.

points.title: Cu Chi & Mekong Both Included
points.body: Both the Cu Chi Tunnels and the Mekong Delta day trip are included in this package. Many operators offer these as add-ons — we include them as core itinerary days.

points.title: Buy 3 Free 1 Promotion
points.body: Groups of 4 pay for 3 — the 4th person travels free. Ask about this offer at booking.

sections.heading: 4 Day Saigon Mekong Delta Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
A 4-day Saigon and Mekong Delta tour from Malaysia is the most popular South Vietnam package for Malaysian travellers — combining Ho Chi Minh City's urban culture and war history with the river life of the Mekong Delta in a four-day itinerary that stays in a single hotel base. The Cu Chi Tunnels are 40 kilometres northwest of the city; the Mekong Delta gateway at My Tho is 70 kilometres south — both are day trips that return to Ho Chi Minh City in the evening.

Ho Chi Minh City has the best street food in Vietnam by most accounts: pho and banh mi are everywhere, but the city's specialities — broken rice (com tam), banh cuon (steamed rice rolls), and the seafood restaurants around the Ben Thanh area — are what serious food travellers come for. The city also has the best coffee culture in Vietnam, with the Café Apartment on Nguyen Hue housing over 20 independent cafés in a single building.

faq.heading: Questions About This 4-Day South Vietnam Package

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. Arrive at Tan Son Nhat International Airport, pass through immigration with your Malaysian passport — no advance application required.

items.question: Are the Cu Chi Tunnels safe to visit?
items.answer: Yes. The tourist areas of the Cu Chi Tunnels have been widened from the original 40cm x 50cm wartime dimensions to accommodate visitors. The section open to tourists (approximately 100 metres long) has been modified for safety. The tunnel experience is confined and low-ceilinged, so visitors with claustrophobia may prefer to observe from the entrance. The outdoor areas and museum exhibitions are fully accessible.

items.question: Is the Mekong Delta tour worthwhile?
items.answer: Yes — the Mekong Delta day trip from Saigon is consistently one of the highest-rated experiences for first-time visitors to South Vietnam. The boat ride through coconut-palm waterways, the tropical fruit tasting, the traditional folk music, and the cottage industry visits in Ben Tre provide a picture of Vietnamese rural life that is completely different from the city. My Tho and Ben Tre are the closest delta towns to Saigon and therefore the most practical for a day trip without losing a full day to transit.

items.question: What is included in the Mekong Delta boat tour?
items.answer: The Mekong Delta day trip includes: return transport from Ho Chi Minh City to My Tho, boat ride from My Tho pier to Thoi Son Island, tropical fruit tasting, honey tea and folk music, sampan paddle under coconut palms, lunch at a local restaurant, and visits to Ben Tre coconut candy and rice paper production workshops. All entrance fees for these activities are included in the package price.
